content:
<sentence>The moon orchid, *Phalaenopsis amabilis*, exhibits a captivating and iconic floral pattern.</sentence>
<sentence>Its most striking feature is the prominent lip, also known as the labellum, which is highly modified and often elaborately shaped.</sentence>
<sentence>This labellum typically displays three lobes, with the middle lobe usually being the largest and most complex.</sentence>
<sentence>Surrounding the lip are typically three petals, two lateral petals and one dorsal petal, all of which are broad and often somewhat rounded.</sentence>
<sentence>Beneath these, two sepals mirror the shape and size of the petals, completing the outer whorl of floral structures.</sentence>
<sentence>The overall arrangement creates a symmetrical, almost butterfly-like or moth-like visual impression.</sentence>
<sentence>Within the flower, a central column houses the reproductive organs, often a contrasting color and texture.</sentence>
<sentence>The surface of the petals and sepals is usually smooth, showcasing delicate veining that adds subtle detail.</sentence>
<sentence>Colors can range from pure white to shades of pink and yellow, with spotting or streaking sometimes present on the lip or petals.</sentence>
<sentence>This combination of a prominent, uniquely shaped lip paired with broad, symmetrical petals and sepals defines the signature pattern of the moon orchid.</sentence>
